lastSession.summary: Resolved scroll performance investigation. Reverted problematic Chromium flags (disable-frame-rate-limit, disable-gpu-vsync) that were dropping FPS to 50. Applied final round of performance fixes: removed FPS counter RAF loop, replaced animate-pulse with compositor-isolated pane-pulse animation, added overscroll-behavior: contain to scroll containers, flattened Workspace flex nesting from 6 to 4 levels. Committed full Electron migration + feature recovery + performance work and pushed to remote.

pendingWork: Remaining scroll momentum settling stutter is a known Chromium CVDisplayLink bug (Issue 40202100) — not fixable at app level, waiting on upstream CADisplayLink migration
Consider monitoring Chromium/Electron releases for CADisplayLink fix
useCodeMirror.ts was deleted (dead code from Ace migration)

recentDecisions.what: Upgraded Electron from 33.4.11 to 40.6.0 to fix macOS 26 Tahoe WindowServer GPU bug (electron/electron#48311). This was the single biggest performance improvement — went from 20fps feel to near-native.
recentDecisions.why: Electron 33 had _cornerMask override that forced WindowServer to re-render masks per-window per-frame on macOS 26

recentDecisions.what: Switching from CodeMirror 6 to React Ace for editor component
recentDecisions.why: CodeMirror not delivering refined editing experience. React Ace provides Cloud9-level quality at 500KB vs Monaco 5-10MB. Right balance for Pane.

recentProblems.description: Compiled main.mjs has CommonJS shims that conflict with ESM build
electron-vite still processing copied main.js despite configuration

recentLessons: Research on scroll momentum settling stutter in Electron on macOS ProMotion 120Hz displays revealed multiple root causes: 1) macOS ProMotion VRR drops from 120Hz to 60Hz during momentum scroll deceleration phase as Apple's adaptive refresh rate conserves power when input velocity decreases, 2) Chromium's CVDisplayLink doesn't properly handle VRR transitions causing frame pacing hitches (CADisplayLink fix in progress), 3) Electron's _cornerMask override caused WindowServer GPU spikes on macOS 26 (fixed in Electron 38.2.0+), 4) CSS layout patterns with nested flex + min-height: 0 can create non-fast scrollable regions forcing main-thread scroll fallback

Electron scroll performance on macOS ProMotion 120Hz: the remaining momentum settling stutter is a known Chromium bug (Issue 40202100) — CVDisplayLink doesn't handle VRR refresh rate tier transitions cleanly during scroll deceleration. This is NOT fixable at the app level. Key fixes that DID help: (1) Remove continuous RAF loops (FPS counter was worst offender), (2) Replace Tailwind animate-pulse with compositor-isolated animation (will-change: opacity + contain: strict), (3) Add overscroll-behavior: contain to scroll containers, (4) Flatten flex nesting depth to reduce non-fast scrollable regions, (5) Remove all custom scrollbar CSS, (6) Remove will-change: transform from scroll containers. Flags that made things WORSE: disable-frame-rate-limit and disable-gpu-vsync dropped FPS to 50.
